parameters.name: Crew O2 consumption per crew-member-day
parameters.source: NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (2022), Table 4-1 nominal; Table 3-31 gives 0.895
parameters.notes: 82 kg reference CM, exercising to hold mass. Range = lower/upper design bounds (~5th-percentile female to 95th-percentile male, ~+/-25%). Legacy value 0.816 kg/CM-d.

parameters.name: Crew CO2 production per crew-member-day
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 4-1 (nominal 1.08); Table 3-31 CO2 load 1.085; RQ 0.86
parameters.notes: 1.04 = O2*RQ*(44/32) consistent point estimate; BVAD Table 4-1 nominal is 1.08. Carry with O2 as a coupled pair.

parameters.name: Respiratory quotient (mol CO2 / mol O2)
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 3-31 (Ewert 2021 MetMan)
parameters.notes: 0.86 rest/sleep, 0.95 aerobic exercise, 0.96 resistive exercise.

parameters.name: Crew total metabolic heat load
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 3-31
parameters.notes: = 143.8 W average per CM. Sensible 6.308 MJ/CM-d (73.0 W), latent 6.118 MJ/CM-d (70.8 W). Sensible->cabin air, latent->condensing HX.

parameters.name: Crew total human water consumption (drink + food prep)
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 4-20 (drinking 2.00 + food rehydration 0.50)
parameters.notes: Table 3-31 potable water content 3.217 kg/CM-d includes 0.717 kg exercise-perspiration allowance. Drinking alone 2.0 kg/CM-d.

parameters.name: Crew hygiene water use (mission-class dependent)
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 4-20
parameters.notes: ISS/transit minimal hygiene 0.7 (urinal flush 0.3 + personal hygiene 0.4). Early planetary base 1.98; mature base 6.62 (adds hand wash, shower, laundry, dish wash). verified correction: BVAD Table 4-20 lists hand wash as TBD/n-a for planetary bases; the 4.08 kg/CM-d hand-wash figure is a WASTEWATER stream (Table 4-21), not a Table 4-20 usage total. Do not add it to hygiene-usage totals.

parameters.name: Crew total water use (excl. payload)
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 4-20
parameters.notes: Transit vehicle 3.2; early planetary base 4.48; mature base 9.12; ISS 5.38 incl 2.18 payload.

parameters.name: Crew urine wastewater output (urine + flush)
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 4-21 (ISS: urine 1.20 + flush 0.30)
parameters.notes: Table 3-31 urine water 1.420 kg/CM-d. Transit/base flush raises total to 1.8-2.0 kg/CM-d.

parameters.name: Crew latent humidity condensate output
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 4-21
parameters.notes: Latent load removed by condensing heat exchanger, feeds WPA. Table 3-31 respiration+perspiration water is 2.946 kg/CM-d.

parameters.name: Cabin total pressure (setpoint options)
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 4-1; NASA exploration atmosphere studies
parameters.notes: 101.3 kPa sea-level (ISS); 70.3 kPa (10.2 psia) and 56.5 kPa (8.2 psia, 34% O2) reduced-pressure exploration options for shorter EVA prebreathe.

parameters.name: Cabin oxygen partial pressure (nominal setpoint)
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 4-1; Norcross 2013; NASA-STD-3001
parameters.notes: Nominal ~21.2 kPa (Earth normal). Measurable impairment until acclimatized 17.2-18.6; min allowable <=1 h is 15.2-17.2 kPa. Upper bounded by flammability at higher O2 fraction.

parameters.name: Cabin nitrogen partial pressure (101.3 kPa atmosphere)
parameters.source: Derived: total 101.3 - ppO2 21.2 - ppCO2 ~0.4 - pH2O ~1.6
parameters.notes: Buffer gas ~78 mol%. Non-consumed except leakage and airlock/EVA losses; makeup dominated by airlock cycling, not metabolism.

parameters.name: Cabin CO2 partial pressure limit (NASA-STD-3001, 1-h avg)
parameters.source: NASA-STD-3001 Vol 2 Rev D [V2 6004]; NASA OCHMO-TB-004
parameters.notes: 3 mmHg = 0.40 kPa 1-h average limit (headache <1.4%). 7-day <2.5 mmHg (0.33 kPa) for <1% headache. ISS observed band 1-9 mmHg (0.13-1.2 kPa). BVAD design nominal 0.507 kPa. Formal CO2 SMAC deleted.

parameters.name: Cabin temperature setpoint
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 4-1
parameters.notes: ~23 C nominal, 18-27 C range. Relative humidity nominal 40% (range 25-75%).

parameters.name: Habitat atmosphere leakage rate (per module, nominal design)
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 4-1, Sec 4.1
parameters.notes: Nominal = 2x the ~0.01 kg/day observed in module test (Gateway-derived margin). As fraction: 0.01-0.14 %/day. Whole-ISS observed leakage ~0.195 kg/day (ESA). verified correction: whole-ISS 'observed 0.195 kg/day (ESA)' is unverified; NASA trending gives ~0.18-0.23 kg/day (2008-2011). Post-2019 Zvezda structural-crack leakage is far higher (~0.45-1.7 kg/day) and not representative of design leakage. Design value 0.02 kg/day/module is unaffected.

parameters.name: OGA maximum O2 production rate (PEM electrolysis)
parameters.source: ICES-2023-311, Status of the Advanced OGA (NTRS 20230008013)
parameters.notes: 20.4 lb/day at 46.9 A max stack current; supports 10.88 CM at 0.85 kg O2/CM-d. Selectable rate; standby 1.0 A. Cumulative 2007-2023: 11,903 kg O2, 1,488 kg H2.

parameters.name: OGA electrolysis specific energy (cell stack)
parameters.source: Derived from ICES-2023-311 (46.9 A, ~1.7 V/cell, ~28 cells => 2.23 kW at 9.25 kg/day)
parameters.notes: Stack level 5.8 kWh/kg (thermoneutral min 4.97 at 1.48 V/cell). System level ~7-12 kWh/kg incl balance-of-plant and part-load; flight estimates ~2.4 kW for ~6-crew O2.

parameters.name: CDRA (4BMS) CO2 removal capacity
parameters.unit: kg/day CO2 per CDRA (20.4 SCFM nominal flow, 2.0 torr inlet ppCO2)
parameters.source: Knox & Stanley, ICES-2015-165, NTRS 20150016500, Sec. II & Table 3 (PW 3.17 kg/day @461 W; PF 3.49 kg/day @486 W at 20 SCFM / 2 torr)
parameters.notes: 4.1 CM-eq at 2.0 torr; 10.5 CM-eq at 5.0 torr; roughly linear in inlet ppCO2. Nominal air flow 34.7 m3/h (20.4 SCFM). ISS 6-CM CO2 throughput ~6.3 kg/day. Per-unit design 6-7 CM. verified correction: the 4.1/10.5 CM-eq capacities were demonstrated in a HIGH-FLOW 25 SCFM, 90-min half-cycle config (+200 W heater); nominal-flow (20.4 SCFM) measured removal is only 3.17-3.49 kg CO2/day (~3.0-3.4 CM-eq), so the prior capacity curve overstated nominal CDRA capacity by ~20-25%. Use 4.1/10.5 CM-eq ONLY for the high-flow configuration.

parameters.name: CDRA nominal power (order of magnitude)
parameters.source: Estimate: heaters + blower + valves; +200 W heater at high-flow config (NTRS 20150016500)
parameters.notes: Open literature gives flow/capacity cleanly but not a single clean nominal power. Thermal-swing heater power dominates and is duty-cycle dependent.

parameters.name: Sabatier CO2-reduction O2-loop closure (metabolic O2 recovered)
parameters.source: NTRS 20100033195 Sabatier CRA; NASA SCOR
parameters.notes: H2-limited: OGA yields 2 H2 per O2 but Sabatier needs 4 H2 per CO2; CH4 vented. ISS estimated ~47%. Water output ~907 kg/yr (7-CM sizing). >50% needs CH4 pyrolysis/Bosch (not operational).

parameters.name: Water Recovery System overall recovery fraction (UPA+WPA+BPA)
parameters.source: NASA water recovery milestone, 20 Jun 2023
parameters.notes: 98% demonstrated with Brine Processor Assembly (2023); 93-94% pre-BPA baseline. Use 98% best-case closed loop, 93% conservative.

parameters.name: UPA urine water recovery fraction (de-ratable)
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Sec 4.3.3 (Carter); Wikipedia ISS ECLSS
parameters.notes: US segment design 85%; de-rated to ~70% on orbit due to calcium-sulfate precipitation in high-Ca urine. Russian ~70%. UPA capacity 9 kg/day (6-CM). Treat as de-ratable parameter.

parameters.name: WPA expendable filter/bed resupply mass
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Sec 4.3.3 (Carrasquillo 2005)
parameters.notes: Multifiltration beds + ion-exchange bed. Drives logistics for closed-loop water on no-resupply missions.

parameters.name: Total ISS ECLSS average electrical power
parameters.source: NASA BVAD Rev2 Table 3-2
parameters.notes: Whole ECLSS average for ISS-class crew. Thermal Control System separately 8.72 kW. OGA is the largest single ECLSS draw.

parameters.name: Regenerative ECLSS hardware mass per crew (ESM sizing estimate)
parameters.source: Estimate: engineering ESM order-of-magnitude incl racks + spares
parameters.notes: Individual ISS ORU masses are not cleanly partitioned in open literature; AOGA cell-stack module alone ~136 kg. Use as placeholder for system-level trades; refine per architecture. verified correction: the ~136 kg (300 lb) launch-config mass is the LEGACY ISS OGA Hydrogen ORU (per ICES-2023-311), not a next-gen AOGA module; the AOGA redesign is intended to reduce it. Do not use 136 kg as a next-gen mass assumption.

modeling_notes: Primary source is NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (Feb 2022, NTRS 20210024855) for all human-interface values; hardware from ISS-heritage ICES papers and NASA OCHMO/NASA-STD-3001. Metabolic O2/CO2/heat scale ~+/-25% (5th-percentile female to 95th-percentile male) and shift with activity via RQ (0.86 rest -> 0.95-0.96 exercise); model crew load as activity-block-weighted, not a single scalar. O2 and CO2 are coupled through RQ; water inflows (potable 3.22 + food 0.76 + metabolic 0.49) balance outflows (urine 1.42 + resp/persp 2.95 + fecal 0.10). Closure economics: OGA electrolysis + Sabatier recovers only ~48% of metabolic O2 (H2-limited); full O2-loop closure needs CH4 pyrolysis (not flight-operational). WRS reaches 98% water closure with the 2023 Brine Processor Assembly (93-94% before). For a Mars no-resupply mission the binding trade is reliability/spares/crew-time (ISS OGA/CDRA/UPA all have recurring failure modes), not steady-state efficiency; UPA recovery in particular de-rates 85%->70% from urine chemistry. Mars ISRU can supply O2 and buffer gas locally, changing whether to close loops or resupply. Fidelity tiers: L0 constant per-CM scalars for mass-balance sizing; L1 activity-block daily model with atmosphere ODEs and hardware capacity as f(ppCO2, production rate); L2 sub-sol physics with adsorption isotherms (CDRA), electrolysis polarization curves (OGA), Sabatier H2-limited kinetics, condensing-HX latent/sensible split, and a nodal water network with de-ratable recovery efficiencies. Cross-couples to thermal (sensible/latent heat), power (OGA/CDRA/Sabatier draw), and logistics/ISRU (O2/N2/water/spares).
key_findings: BVAD Rev2 reference crew (82 kg): O2 consumed ~0.90 kg/CM-d, CO2 produced ~1.04-1.085 kg/CM-d at RQ 0.86, metabolic heat 12.43 MJ/CM-d (143.8 W; ~51% sensible / 49% latent). Design spread ~+/-25%.
Water: total human consumption 2.5 kg/CM-d (2.0 drink + 0.5 food prep); hygiene 0.7 kg/CM-d ISS-minimal up to 6.62 for a mature base; urine wastewater 1.5 kg/CM-d; humidity condensate 2.27 kg/CM-d.

Atmosphere: 101.3 kPa sea-level baseline (ppO2 ~21.2, ppN2 ~79 kPa) with 70.3/56.5 kPa reduced-pressure exploration options; CO2 held below NASA-STD-3001 limit of 3 mmHg (0.40 kPa) 1-h average; formal CO2 SMAC has been deleted.
Leakage baselined at 0.02 kg/day/module (2x the ~0.01 measured), i.e. up to ~0.14 %/day of cabin gas; whole-ISS observed ~0.195 kg/day.
OGA (PEM electrolysis) makes up to 9.25 kg O2/day at 46.9 A (supports 10.88 CM); derived stack specific energy ~5.8 kWh/kg O2 (system ~7-12 incl balance-of-plant).
CDRA (4-bed molecular sieve) removes 4.1 CM-equiv of CO2 at 2.0 torr inlet (10.5 CM-eq at 5.0 torr) at 34.7 m3/h flow; nominal power ~0.6-1.0 kW (heater-dominated, estimate).
Sabatier recovers only ~47-48% of metabolic O2 from CO2 (H2-limited, CH4 vented); full O2-loop closure requires methane pyrolysis not yet operational.

Water Recovery System reaches 98% overall closure with the 2023 Brine Processor Assembly (93-94% before); UPA urine recovery is 85% design but de-rates to ~70% on orbit from calcium-sulfate precipitation.

Total ISS ECLSS average power ~5.31 kW; for a 3-year Mars mission spares mass and crew-maintenance time (OGA cell-stack/pump, CDRA valves, UPA chemistry) dominate the ECLSS logistics trade over steady-state efficiency.
